column1: C[Cosha11: Sorry, explain how you get points again... I think I'm missing that slight bit. So its not apparently how many pieces you capture, but how man""Whats"" you get?
xentac: when you finish a game, there are spaces that you've surrounded
xentac: you count the empty spaces
xentac: the prisoners themselves are subtracted from your empty space score
xentac: so your goal is to surround the largest territory
C[Cosha11: It's deceiving to tell if your online or not
Cosha11: And tell me where you have 0.5 points?
Cosha11: Unless it spoils the game in any way
xentac: the 0.5 is just to split ties
xentac: I gave you 5 stones to start
xentac: so I get an extra 0.5, so we can't have any ties
xentac: I have a little green circle beside my name that says I'm online
xentac: but I might not actually be at the computer...
C[Cosha11: Can't you just leave me guys alone, and go put your guys in a corner or somewhere?
C[xentac: Nah, it's more fun to try and capture them!
C[xentac: that move wasn't so good...
xentac: now this situation is a little bit weird
xentac: it looks like you'd be able to capture right back
xentac: it seems like you'd be able to play H7
xentac: you can't play in the spot I just captured, if it'll put us back to where we were before
C[xentac: so you have to play somewhere else
xentac: actually, that's a great ko threat
xentac: if I ignored your play, you could have captured my E8 stone
xentac: which would have been really bad for me
xentac: so I had to play something like F9 to save myself
xentac: which lets you play H7 now
xentac: this is how a ko fight goes
C[xentac: we are just about done
xentac: also, shouldn't you be sleeping? :P
C[Cosha11: But I could ask the same
xentac: I don't have a bed time, I'm an adult!
C[xentac: uh oh, you lose the ko
xentac: well, the ko is just the capturing back and forth
xentac: you can't do it anymore
Cosha11: Well I know why I lost it, but how?
xentac: the reason you lost it was because you made a move that I didn't have to respond to
C[xentac: which gave me the opportunity to end the ko
xentac: you should have gone with something like D8
xentac: and dragged it out more
xentac: you had more ko threats than I did
xentac: in fact, my J4 was barely a threat
xentac: but you fell for it!
Cosha11: So if I go to B6, you wouldnt be able to play on A5 right?
Cosha11: Or would you just capture and I wouldnt be able to play there...
C[Cosha11: So if I play at B9, you would be able to play A9?
xentac: Sorry, I'm playing on my phone and didn't notice your typing.
C[xentac: It's a lot harder when you don't have handicap pieces to fall back to
C[Cosha11: What was the saying you used?
C[Cosha11: I GOT ONE I GOT ONE!!
xentac: You could have passed a long time ago
C[xentac: I got all the points and you got none of them...
Cosha11: One more with no handicap, then we'll bump it up if I dont improve
xentac: I don't think it's worth it, to be honest. You have to understand life and death and eyes better before then.
xentac: We could try 3 or 4 handicap. And you might also want to choose the more traditional starting position.
xentac: Which would you prefer 3 or 4?
Cosha11: Lets go with 4 then (is that traditional?)
xentac: I mean there are traditional placements for the handicap stones. No matter how many there are.
Cosha11: Alrighty, Ill google them and use them for next round
C[saya: hi. good game, have fun :)
C[saya: hm this is not going well for me
C[saya: are you 30k or just not ranked yet?
C[Cosha11: Well I had a good teacher :)
Cosha11: I havent been playing long though
C[Cosha11: If it enlightens you, your my first opponent other than my mentor
C[saya: I'm honored. I think you are better than me, although I've been playing many people (long games) since ~January
saya: long games sometimes make it hard to learn I think
Cosha11: Well what my mentor does it review it with me, did you want to do that in the end?
C[saya: I'm not giving up yet
C[Cosha11: Well, at this point placing stones I dont need to respond to is giving me points
Cosha11: But if you can survive on my side, go for it then
C[saya: now you have two eyes and I can't make more, so I'm happy. ready to pass and finish?
C[xentac: I don't know why they let you choose the handicap placement
xentac: this is the traditional positions
Cosha11: Maybe on the way the game was set up?
C[xentac: I thought that japanese rules forced you to have a fixed layout...
C[Cosha11: So to take a piece of yours, diagonal doesn't count as touching right?
xentac: that's correct, only the lines coming off of it count
C[Cosha11: So let me get this straight...
Cosha11: If you put a piece on A3, you get all the guys on the left-ish
xentac: but you'd have to make the group alive...
C[Cosha11: And If I got to a7, I get all your men?
xentac: how many lines are coming off that group?
xentac: even if you went to a7, you'd still have to go to a9, b9, and c9
C[xentac: how many liberties does your B7 group have now?
Cosha11: And if you go b6, you get them all.....
xentac: liberties are only counted as open spaces
xentac: once a group has no liberties, it's captured
xentac: so you had that part right
Cosha11: So if I go to b6, you get the group?
xentac: I don't think you're allowed to go to B6
xentac: because you're not allowed to capture yourself
xentac: there aren't any more moves to make
xentac: you can't threaten anything of mine
xentac: I can't threaten anything of yours
xentac: which is pretty good for you actually
xentac: I expected you to have a lot more trouble
C[Cosha11: Oh, thanks for the lesson by the way :)
xentac: I especially like saying, ""that move is stupid""
Cosha11: Well as long as its not boring
xentac: I like making you think
Cosha11: I THOUGHT ABOUT IT TO!
C[Cosha11: See what I did there?
C[xentac: there you go, I gave you the undo
xentac: it'll be hard to make it live
xentac: either by capturing or making it out
C[Cosha11: Was that a good move? Since its not an empty triangle right...
xentac: it is not an empty triangle, this is true
C[Cosha11: Hey, is there a time limit on our Go game?
xentac: yeah, you have 15 days + 2 weeks and 6 days per 20 moves
C[Cosha11: Well Im leaving for vacation tomorrow...
C[xentac: We shall see if you run out of time or not.  Usually they have vacation days that kick in.
xentac: is there anything you can attack?
xentac: or that you need to defend?
xentac: but you have to make the decision yourself
Cosha11: Well you get points for pieces you capture right?
xentac: but every piece you play is a lost point
xentac: those stones are so yummy
C[xentac: you should have known they were dead at move 24 or 25
Cosha11: And if so, I should play D9, or should that be automatic
xentac: you don't have to play anything
C[xentac: if it doesn't have two eyes or couldn't get them, it's dead
xentac: well, now you just lost yourself a point
C[xentac: would you like to lose more?
C[xentac: ok, you mark the dead stones
xentac: we might have to go to 3 stones soon
Cosha11: So by playing d9, I lost a point
xentac: because those stones were dead
Cosha11: And it would have been concidered dead already
xentac: you would have had an extra space at d9
xentac: my score right now is 17.5
xentac: how many spaces do I have
xentac: 12 not including the black one
xentac: I captured 3 in the game
Cosha11: but what about that dead one?
xentac: so the black stone at C3 is also considered captured
xentac: and the space it took up in mine
Cosha11: Or is it counted by a space
Cosha11: I think I get it
xentac: can you mouse over those ones?
Cosha11: So 12 for spaces, 4 for deads...
xentac: because the C3 space is empty after we remove the dead stone
Cosha11: Ah, NOW I get it
Cosha11: Until I beat you there
Cosha11: And then just go down till theres none
Cosha11: And you can decide when you want to use the big board
xentac: actually, before then we'll probably go to the big board
xentac: sometime around 3 or 2 handicap stones
xentac: and then we'll play on a board where you'll get 9
Cosha11: Why, you act as if that may take awhile to beat you with no handicap
xentac: yes, it's a very different game
xentac: if you can beat me with no handicap, I would expect you to be able to do some serious damage against me on a big board
xentac: but the strategy on a small board is different than a big one
xentac: think of the 9x9 as the kiddie pool
Cosha11: Or can you transfer over stuff youve learned here
xentac: would you rather be able to do laps in the kiddie pool?
xentac: or would you like to swim sooner in the big pool?
xentac: being awesome at 9x9 isn't the goal, being awesome at 19x19 is
xentac: we'll drop down to 3 handicap next
C[xentac: you just connected a piece to a dead group...
Cosha11: Can I save the guys at the bottem with eyes?
xentac: you have to be careful
C[xentac: normally you save a move like that till the end
xentac: but in this case, I sort of have to respond to it
C[Cosha11: But then you could just do the same thing to me first...
Cosha11: Or does it not affect the amount of points for either side
xentac: It does, but it's a small number of points compared to other moves you could make on the board
xentac: you always want to think what the best move is, not just what a move is
C[Cosha11: Sorry, even still this rules gets me, if I play d8, you would STILL be able to kill e7? EVEN THOUGH, its ""kinda"" looks like suicide
C[xentac: yes, I would be able to play d8, because my move would be capturing and opening up a liberty at e7... it's a ko
Cosha11: If I try like crazy to save g3, the result of me placing my men gives you more points in the end?
xentac: either you can make something that lives
xentac: in that case, all the empty space is yours and not mine
xentac: if you place a stone and I respond
xentac: as in, no points are gained or lost
Cosha11: So its worth a shot to try and save it?
xentac: the only way you would lose points
xentac: is if you played and I didn't respond
xentac: so you have to be careful that every piece you play is necessary for life
xentac: if you played a move that I didn't need to respond to, then it's free points for me
Cosha11: So as long as you place a stone to respond, its worth nothing?
Cosha11: Since if we passed right now, you'd win eh?
xentac: well... you need to protect something first...
xentac: but I think I would win
C[xentac: ok, look at what you just did
xentac: the group had how many liberties before?
xentac: the one you just extended
Cosha11: One, and it still has one
Cosha11: Just pass one more time please :)
xentac: so what did that move gain you?
xentac: it wouldn't have gained you anything
xentac: even if I had passed
Cosha11: If I played at f3
Cosha11: Then I'd eat all those guys and win
Cosha11: BUT since you didnt pass....
xentac: I could have played in so many ways to protect that
Cosha11: But I was one move away
xentac: it would have been 7.5, but I passed once
xentac: do we need to go over this one?
xentac: I don't see many mistakes, apart from maybe not trying to save your stone sooner
Cosha11: Well I think I got it, unless you want to point anything out
xentac: also, trying to split me is a good thing
xentac: let's go over a couple things
C[Cosha11: I hate it when you do that
Cosha11: Take one without my knowing of the move before hand
Cosha11: And one I could have prevented
xentac: well, you should have seen it
xentac: once I played F8, it was inevitable
Cosha11: Could have least given me a hint...
C[xentac: if you'd like that, I can do that next time
C[Cosha11: No, all in good fun
Cosha11: Are you seeing something that im not?
Cosha11: Since all i see is J7
xentac: how do you stop the J6 group from being captured?
xentac: I thought when you said you ""saw it comming"" you meant that you protected yourself
Cosha11: No, I was meaning h6 D:
xentac: but then you played J5
xentac: even though it didn't help you
xentac: you basically gave up another piece
xentac: that combination of moves is a very common end game one
C[xentac: another way to think about it though... you never took any steps to save him...
C[Cosha11: The concept of eyes is comming in handy
xentac: eyes are very key... once a group has eyes, it's uncapturable
xentac: that's how you prove to everyone that something is your territory
C[xentac: unless a group has two eyes, it's dead
C[Cosha11: How many eyes do you need?
xentac: one eye is not enough
xentac: I can show you later
C[Cosha11: Should I be passing now?
xentac: yeah, good, you figured out where you needed to play
C[xentac: sadly, that wasn't the right play
C[xentac: mmmm... the taste of your tears
Cosha11: You didnt see what I was doing?
xentac: no... I think you're confused
xentac: if you're trying to make eyes... you have lots of opportunity still
xentac: komi is the 0.5 I get at the beginning
xentac: you put the D3 group in atari
Cosha11: So would it be better to TRY and save it? or just go do something else
xentac: well, no matter what you lose E2
xentac: so given that, what is the state of your other pieces?
Cosha11: If I play e1, it would end up as dead, through 2-3 moves
xentac: and pretend E2 isn't there
xentac: what does that tell you about other pieces?
xentac: that saves E8, for sure
C[xentac: good that you recognized it though
C[Cosha11: So if you didnt play j5, and I went down to j2, and h2, would those be eyes?
xentac: I don't fully understand the question
Cosha11: Ill draw it out after then, since its moot
xentac: the spaces have to be completely surrounded... so who would have played at J5?
Cosha11: leave h4, g4, and h3, open
xentac: if you had a piece at J5, H2 and J2
xentac: if you then played H4
xentac: you would have two eyes
xentac: that would have separated them into two eyes
Cosha11: But now i cant right?
xentac: you just need two separated spaces within there somewhere
xentac: I'm hoping that you can't so I can win
Cosha11: So IF I played h3, j3. would those be eyes?
xentac: if you play H3, G4 and H4 would be one eyes
Cosha11: I have a mic if thats quicker
xentac: I have a call to make at 2
xentac: so I don't have a lot of time
Cosha11: Alrighty, Ill make it quick then
xentac: so you want to use the mic?
xentac: how about we finish it now
xentac: and then maybe review with audio after my call
Cosha11: So eyes have to be seperated
C[xentac: now you're in trouble though
C[Cosha11: I see the mistake I made by not playing A2
xentac: you would have had me
C[xentac: I can't play this one realtime
xentac: but I'll be able to make a couple moves today

xentac: so this might still be alive...

Cosha11: which starts a ko fight

xentac: I'm going to say yes

xentac: no, I'm pretty sure that no matter what happens they're dead
Cosha11: Huzza! It looks like they can live

xentac: so that would have brought our score to a difference of 7.5
xentac: Yeah, I think that wasn't right
C[xentac: Not sure if you want to finish this game tonight, but I probably have time for one full 9x9 tonight
C[xentac: that's ok, there's still more of the board
C[xentac: I think you were screwed either way
